---
title: "4.10 Min, Max, Sum, Average, and Properties"
subtitle: "Implementing ArrayList Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Min, Max, Sum, Average

### Standard traversal algorithms

You should be able to write code that finds the minimum, maximum, sum, and average of the elements in an `ArrayList`.

These algorithms usually use:

- a variable initialized before the loop
- an indexed loop or enhanced `for` loop
- a statement inside the loop that updates the variable
- a return statement after the loop

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: arraylistAvgParsons`

The following method should calculate the average from an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

The blocks have been mixed up and include one extra block that is not needed in a correct solution.

::: {.code-scroll .compact style="max-height: 320px;"}
```java
public static double average(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
=====
   double sum = 0;
=====
   for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
   {
=====
   for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++)
   { // extra block
=====
       sum += nums.get(i);
=====
   } // end for loop
=====
   return (sum / nums.size());
=====
} // end average method
```
:::

## Mixed-Up Code: Correct Order

### arraylistAvgParsons

```java
public static double average(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
   double sum = 0;
   for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
   {
       sum += nums.get(i);
   } // end for loop
   return (sum / nums.size());
} // end average method
```

The extra `nums.length` block belongs to arrays, not `ArrayList`.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: arraylistMinParsons`

The following program segment is a method that should return the minimum `int` given an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

The blocks have been mixed up and include one extra block that is not needed.

::: {.code-scroll .compact style="max-height: 300px;"}
```java
public static int findMin(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
=====
    int min = nums.get(0);
=====
    int min = nums[0]; // extra block
=====
    for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
    {
=====
        if (nums.get(i) < min)
        {
=====
            min = nums.get(i);
=====
        } // end if statement
=====
    } // end for loop
=====
    return min;
=====
} // end findMin method
```
:::

## Mixed-Up Code: Correct Order

### arraylistMinParsons

```java
public static int findMin(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
    int min = nums.get(0);
    for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
    {
        if (nums.get(i) < min)
        {
            min = nums.get(i);
        } // end if statement
    } // end for loop
    return min;
} // end findMin method
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: arraylistMax`

Create a `findMax` method that finds and returns the largest value in an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

## ListMax Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.util.ArrayList;

public class ListMax
{
    public static int findMax(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
    {
        // YOUR CODE HERE //

        return 0;
    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        ArrayList<Integer> list = new ArrayList<Integer>();
        list.add(1);
        list.add(3);
        list.add(12);
        list.add(0);
        list.add(9);

        System.out.println("Max: " + findMax(list));
    }
}
```
:::

## ListMax Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The output from `main` should be:

```text
Max: 12
```

A second test calls `ListMax.findMax(list)` with `[2, 4, 0]`.

Expected return value:

```text
4
```

## Finding a Property

### Use an `if` statement inside the loop

You should be able to write code that determines:

- if at least one element has a particular property
- if all elements have a particular property
- the number of elements having a particular property

In the AP exam, the property is often given as a boolean method for you to use inside the loop.

For example, `isEven(num)` returns `true` if the number is even.

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: arraylistEvenParsons`

The following method should return `true` if at least one element in an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects is even.

The blocks have been mixed up and include extra blocks that are not needed.

::: {.code-scroll .compact style="max-height: 340px;"}
```java
public static boolean hasEven(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
=====
    for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
    {
=====
    for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++)
    { // extra block
=====
        if (nums.get(i) % 2 == 0)
        {
=====
            return true;
=====
        } // end if statement
=====
        else
        {
            return false;
        } // extra block
=====
    } // end for loop
=====
    return false;
=====
} // end hasEven method
```
:::

## Mixed-Up Code: Correct Order

### arraylistEvenParsons

```java
public static boolean hasEven(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
{
    for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++)
    {
        if (nums.get(i) % 2 == 0)
        {
            return true;
        } // end if statement
    } // end for loop
    return false;
} // end hasEven method
```

Do not return `false` inside the loop before checking all elements.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: countOddArrayList`

Write a method `countOdd` that returns the number of odd numbers in an `ArrayList` of `Integer` objects.

## TestCountOdd Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.util.*;

public class TestCountOdd
{
     public static int countOdd(ArrayList<Integer> nums)
     {
          // Add code here
          return 0;
     }

     public static void main(String[] args)
     {
          // instantiate ArrayList and fill with Integers
          ArrayList<Integer> values = new ArrayList<Integer>();
          int[] nums = {1, 5, 7, 9, -2, 3, 2};
          for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++)
          {
            values.add(nums[i]);
          }
          int result = countOdd(values);
          System.out.println("Expected Result:\t 5");
          System.out.println("Your Result:\t\t " + result);
     }
}
```
:::

## TestCountOdd Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The output from `main` should be:

```text
Expected Result: 5
Your Result:     5
```

A second test calls `TestCountOdd.countOdd(list)` with `[2, -2, 1, 11]`.

Expected return value:

```text
2
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- initialize the accumulator or candidate value before the loop
- use `nums.size()` and `nums.get(i)` with an `ArrayList`
- update max/min/sum/count inside the loop
- return the property result only after the algorithm has enough information
- preserve starter code comments such as `// Add code here`

## End

### 4.10 Min, Max, Sum, Average, and Properties

Next: Pairs and Duplicates.
